title: HTTP Client Reuse, Retrieval, Redirects, Validators, and Bounded Bodies · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded HTTP page-fetch contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Reuse one configured client or bounded pool and close it deliberately so connection, proxy, TLS, cookie, and resource ownership are visible.
Use safe retrieval methods and an honest User-Agent, Accept, Accept-Encoding, conditional, and contact policy without impersonation.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, content coding, and domain outcomes before parsing the representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Bounded Streaming
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-bounded-streaming-181k1b" that uses case 3015 to validate client ownership, response admission, redirect revalidation, bounded streaming, and cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Bounded Streaming.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bounded Streaming into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Bounded Streaming, keep Bounded Streaming connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-bounded-streaming-181k1b
# Competency: scraper-bounded-streaming.
# Case 3015: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_bounded_streaming_181k1b(responses: int = 12, admitted_html: int = 8, rejected_bodies: int = 4, redirect_hops: int = 3, leaked_clients: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_181k1b = "181k1b-b1k181-3015"
    assert evidence_variant_181k1b
    evidence_axes_181k1b = (
        "authorization181k1b",
        "url3015",
        "responseb1k181",
        "tree181k1b3015",
        "frontier3015181k1b",
        "reportb1k1813015",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_181k1b)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or admitted_html < 0 or rejected_bodies < 0:
        return False, responses, "response-count"
    if admitted_html + rejected_bodies != responses:
        return False, admitted_html, "admission-reconciliation"
    if redirect_hops < 0 or redirect_hops > 5:
        return False, redirect_hops, "redirect-budget"
    if leaked_clients > 0:
        return False, leaked_clients, "client-cleanup"
    assert admitted_html + rejected_bodies == responses and leaked_clients == 0
    return True, admitted_html, "bounded-page-fetch"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
